prose.en:
# Further information

Further information on implementing opportunistic TLS encryption for email servers can be found in the Australian Signals Directorate’s (ASD) [Implementing certificates, TLS, HTTPS and opportunistic TLS](#b8cdbf18-31cb-4e10-bb43-e8488ee8c2f6) publication.

Further information on implementing SPF, DKIM and DMARC can be found in ASD’s [How to combat fake emails](#d3572f80-d9d6-4023-b6e6-23720bb0d618) publication.

Further information on engaging the services of email service providers for marketing or filtering purposes can be found in ASD’s [Marketing and filtering email service providers](#e4eee462-f6e5-4921-a8fe-26f3de6c6f45) publication.

Further information on email content filtering can be found in the ‘Content filtering’ section of the [Guidelines for gateways](#e8bde527-526c-4a6a-b66f-05228f09dd7b).

Further information on email content filtering can be found in ASD’s [Malicious email mitigation strategies](#6136b137-0cc9-4d21-94ee-0abfac2b4e18) publication.

Further information on email security can be found in the following National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) publications:

- NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-45 Version 2, [Guidelines on Electronic Mail Security](#b383a647-7627-4450-99b6-640ae554c2f1)
- NIST SP 800-177 Rev. 1, [Trustworthy Email](#4b0f2fa7-f9d6-48d8-b14c-527939808882)
- NIST SP 1800-6, [Domain Name System-Based Electronic Mail Security](#55038d94-7587-4234-a18c-fb459fcdb295).
